content.text:
Based on my analysis of the chart, I can now trace the relationships of the black-labeled employees (Ryan Howard, Kelly Kapoor, Darryl Philbin, Val Johnson) across the 2005–2013 timeline.

<think>
**Black-labeled employees:** Ryan Howard (RH), Kelly Kapoor (KK), Darryl Philbin (DP), Val Johnson (VJ)

**Timeline analysis:**
- **Kelly Kapoor (KK):** First in "RH + KK" starting ~2006, then transitions to "DP + KK" (around 2008–2009) while Ryan is single, then back to "RH + KK" through 2013. Kelly seamlessly moves from one relationship to another, with minimal single time after 2006.
- **Ryan Howard (RH):** In "RH + KK" from ~2006 through 2013 with multiple breaks — when Kelly is with Darryl, Ryan is single.
- **Darryl Philbin (DP):** Single until ~2008, briefly with Kelly ("DP + KK"), single again, then with Val ("DP + VJ") from ~2012–2013.
- **Val Johnson (VJ):** Only in "DP + VJ" starting ~2012 — the least relationship time.

The critical distinction: when Kelly switches to Darryl, Ryan is single. So Kelly's total relationship time = Ryan's time with Kelly + her additional time with Darryl. Kelly Kapoor has the most relationship time among the black-labeled employees.
</think>

<answer>
Kelly Kapoor

Among the black-labeled employees (Ryan Howard, Kelly Kapoor, Darryl Philbin, Val Johnson), Kelly Kapoor spent the most time in a relationship. Starting around 2006, she was in "RH + KK" with Ryan Howard, then transitioned to "DP + KK" with Darryl Philbin (around 2008–2009, while Ryan was single), and then returned to "RH + KK" through 2013. This continuous overlap of relationships — essentially moving from one partner to another with minimal single time — means Kelly accumulated more total relationship time than Ryan Howard (who was single during Kelly's time with Darryl), Darryl Philbin (single for most of the early period), or Val Johnson (only in a relationship at the very end).
</answer>
